title: Coding-Decoding
subtitle: Letter Shifts, Number Coding, Mirror Coding & Substitution

readingSections.title: What is Coding-Decoding?
readingSections.content: Coding-Decoding is a reasoning skill where letters, numbers, or symbols are transformed using a hidden rule. Your task is to crack the logic. This is one of the most scoring topics in campus placement exams like TCS, Infosys, Wipro, and Accenture.

subsections.title: Why Companies Ask
subsections.content: Coding-decoding tests your ability to find patterns quickly. In IT jobs, you constantly understand how data transforms from one form to another. This shows recruiters you can: find hidden rules, apply rules consistently, and work systematically under pressure.

subsections.title: Basic Structure
subsections.content: You get a word and its code. Example: CAT is coded as FDW. Find the pattern, then either code another word or decode a given code. All questions follow: find the rule, apply the rule.

subsections.title: Real-Life Example
subsections.content: Think of a secret language where every letter shifts by +3 (A->D, B->E). When friend sends FDW, you decode it as CAT. Companies test how fast you learn the secret language.

subsections.title: Success Strategy
subsections.content: Step 1: Write A=1 to Z=26 on rough paper. Step 2: Compare each original letter with its code. Step 3: Note the difference per position. Step 4: Apply same pattern to new word. Step 5: Verify at least 2 letters before finalizing.

readingSections.title: Letter Coding Types
readingSections.content: Letter coding transforms letters into other letters. This is the most common category.

subsections.title: Forward Shift Coding
subsections.content: Each letter moves forward by fixed number. Example: CAT->FDW (+3 each). C+3=F, A+3=D, T+3=W. Common shifts: +1, +2, +3, +5. If wrapping: Z+1=A (circular alphabet).

subsections.title: Backward Shift Coding
subsections.content: Each letter moves backward. Example: DOG->ALD (-3). D-3=A, O-3=L, G-3=D. For wrapping: A-1=Z.

subsections.title: Mirror/Reverse Coding
subsections.content: A<->Z, B<->Y, C<->X. Formula: new_pos = 27 - old_pos. A(1) becomes Z(26). This is like seeing the alphabet in a mirror.

subsections.title: Alternating Pattern Coding
subsections.content: Different letters get different shifts. Example: LAP->OBS: L+3=O, A+1=B, P+3=S. Always check 2+ letter pairs to identify the alternating rule.

subsections.title: Group Coding
subsections.content: Groups of letters coded together. Example: AB->CD, EF->GH. Or reverse pairs: AB->BA, CD->DC.

readingSections.title: Number and Mixed Coding
readingSections.content: Numbers represent letters through position values. Letters and numbers can mix.

subsections.title: Direct Position Coding
subsections.content: Write the position number: A=1, B=2... CAT = 3-1-20 = 3120. Very common in TCS and Infosys.

subsections.title: Sum/Product of Positions
subsections.content: Positions are added or multiplied. CAT = 3+1+20 = 24 (single number). Check if code length matches word (concatenated) or shorter (sum).

subsections.title: Digit Manipulation
subsections.content: Multiple operations on digits: sum (12->3), reverse (23->32), product (12->2).

subsections.title: Symbol-Based Coding
subsections.content: Symbols like star, circle, #, $ represent letters/numbers. Straightforward substitution.

subsections.title: Mixed Letter-Number Coding
subsections.content: Code has both letters and numbers. Example: GO = G(7)+O(15) = 715. Just concatenate positions.

readingSections.title: Advanced and Company Patterns
readingSections.content: Placement exams often combine multiple patterns.

subsections.title: Conditional Coding
subsections.content: Rule changes based on position or letter type. Vowels get +2, consonants get +3. Or first letter gets +1, last +2, middle +3.

subsections.title: Vowel-Consonant Coding
subsections.content: Different rules for vowels (A,E,I,O,U) and consonants. Identify vowels first, then apply rules.

subsections.title: TCS Coding Patterns
subsections.content: Forward/backward shifts (+3, +5), Mirror coding, Position value concatenation, Alternating patterns. 45 sec per question.

subsections.title: Infosys Coding Patterns
subsections.content: Complex alternating patterns, Vowel-consonant coding, Mixed letter-number. 60 sec per question. Harder.

subsections.title: Wipro/Accenture Patterns
subsections.content: Simple shifts (+1,+2,+3), Direct substitution, Position coding. Easier but faster pace: 30-35 sec.

subsections.title: EJOTY Trick
subsections.content: E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25. Use nearest anchor. Need S? S is 1 before T(20)=19. Need G? G is 2 after E(5)=7.

subsections.title: The 27-Minus Trick
subsections.content: For mirror coding: 27 - position. Memorize one formula. A(1) -> 27-1=26=Z. M(13) -> 27-13=14=N.

subsections.title: Pattern Detection Speed
subsections.content: Check first AND last letter pairs first. If same shift, rule is uniform. If different, check for alternating.

subsections.title: Two-Letter Verification
subsections.content: Never guess from one pair. Always check at least two letter pairs. If they match, you are confident.

subsections.title: Reverse Check for Decoding
subsections.content: To decode, reverse the rule. If coding was +3, decoding is -3. Mirror (27-pos) is its own reverse!

readingSections.title: Common Mistakes
readingSections.content: Learn these to avoid losing easy marks.

subsections.title: Wrong Direction
subsections.content: If coding adds +3, decoding subtracts -3. Always check: original to code or code to original? Write arrows!

subsections.title: Forgetting Wrap Around
subsections.content: Z+1 = A (not Z). A-1 = Z (not A). Use circular alphabet: after Z comes A, before A is Z.

subsections.title: Miscounting Positions
subsections.content: Use EJOTY anchors or write positions. Quick check: A=1, E=5, I=9, O=15, U=21.

subsections.title: Assuming Uniform Pattern
subsections.content: Not all codes use same shift. Check 2-3 pairs. Look for vowel/consonant distinction or position-based rules.

subsections.title: Skipping Verification
subsections.content: Take 5 seconds to verify one letter pair. If the code matches your rule, you are correct. One quick check saves 5 wrong answers.

readingSections.title: Quick Revision
readingSections.content: One-page recap of everything for Coding-Decoding.

subsections.title: 30-Second Revision
subsections.content: Forward = Add. Backward = Subtract. Mirror = 27-P. Position = A=1 to Z=26. Substitution = Direct mapping. Always check 2+ pairs.

subsections.title: Interview Tips
subsections.content: This is one of the fastest-scoring topics. Most questions solve in 20-30 seconds with practice. Focus on: speed position finding (EJOTY), pattern detection (check 2 letters), and careful arithmetic (wrap-around). Practice 20 daily questions for automaticity.

formulas.title: Alphabet Position Value
formulas.explanation: Foundation of all coding-decoding. Each letter has a fixed position. Memorize using EJOTY trick: E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25.

formulas.title: Forward Letter Shift
formulas.explanation: Each letter shifts forward by a fixed number. Same shift for all or alternating.
formulas.example: CAT -> FDW (+3 each): C+3=F, A+3=D, T+3=W

formulas.title: Backward Letter Shift
formulas.explanation: Each letter shifts backward. Handle wrapping: A-1 = Z.
formulas.example: DOG -> ALD (-3 each): D-3=A, O-3=L, G-3=D

formulas.title: Reverse/Mirror Coding
formulas.explanation: A(1) -> Z(26), B(2) -> Y(25). Just subtract from 27.

formulas.title: Direct Substitution Coding
formulas.explanation: Each letter maps to a specific code letter. May be random but consistent.
formulas.example: If EARTH=QWERT then E->Q, A->W, R->E, T->R, H->T

formulas.title: Mixed Letter-Number Coding
formulas.explanation: Write position number of each letter and join. CAT -> C(3)A(1)T(20) -> 3120.

formulas.title: Symbol to Value Coding
formulas.explanation: Symbols (like stars, circles, #, $) are assigned values.

formulas.title: EJOTY Quick Reference
formulas.explanation: Use these 5 anchor points for quick position finding.
formulas.example: Find M: M is 2 before O(15) -> 13. Find R: R is 3 after O(15) -> 18

letter-shift-backward.q: If BALL = A Z K K (each -1), then CALL = ?

s: Step 1: Each letter shifts +1: A->B, P->Q, P->Q, L->M, E->F.
Step 2: B->C, A->B, N->O, A->B, N->O, A->B.
Step 1: S+2=U, T+2=V, A+2=C, R+2=T. All +2.

substitution-coding.q: If PINK = QJOL and BALL = CBMM, then RING = ?

s: Step 1: L->M, A->B, P->Q, T->U, O->P, P->Q. All +1.
Step 2: M->N, O->P, B->C, I->J, L->M, E->F.

mixed-coding.q: If GO = 715 and TO = 2015, then GOT = ?
If SUN = 192114 and MOON = 13151514, then MOONLIT = ?

symbol-coding.q: If star = 1, circle = 2, triangle = 3, then star-circle-triangle = ?
If alpha-beta-gamma = 123 and beta-gamma-alpha = 231, then gamma-alpha-beta = ?
If A=*, B=#, C=$, then A+B+C = ?

mcqs.exp: Step 1: C=3, A=1, B=2. Step 2: Concatenate as 312. Step 3: 312 correct.
Step 1: Each letter +3: C->F, A->D, T->W. Step 2: D+3=G, O+3=R, G+3=J. Step 3: GRJ correct.
Step 1: A(1) maps to Z(26) by 27-pos. Step 2: B(2)->27-2=25=Y. Step 3: Y correct.
Step 1: All letters +1: A->B,P->Q,P->Q,L->M,E->F. Step 2: B->C,A->B,N->O,A->B,N->O,A->B. Step 3: CBOBOB correct.
Step 1: S-3=P,U-3=R,N-3=K. Step 2: M-3=J,O-3=L,O-3=L,N-3=K. Step 3: JLLK correct.

mcqs.q: If GO=715 and TO=2015, then GOT=?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: G=7,O=15,T=20. Step 2: GOT=71520. Step 3: 71520 correct.
Step 1: B->Y,A->Z,L->O,L->O (27-pos). Step 2: C->X,A->Z,L->O,L->O. Step 3: XZOO correct.
Step 1: T+1=U,O+1=P,P+1=Q. Step 2: B+1=C,O+1=P,T+1=U. Step 3: CPU correct.
Step 1: S+2=U,T+2=V,A+2=C,R+2=T. Step 2: M+2=O,O+2=Q,O+2=Q,N+2=P. Step 3: OQQP correct.
Step 1: C=3,A=1,B=2. Step 2: B=2,A=1,C=3->213. Step 3: 213 correct.
Step 1: 1=A,2=B,3=C. Step 2: 321=CBA. Step 3: CBA correct.
Step 1: H+3=K,A+2=C,T+3=W -> +3,+2,+3. Step 2: S+3=V,U+2=W,N+3=Q. Step 3: VWQ correct.
Step 1: L+3=O,A+1=B,P+3=S -> +3,+1,+3. Step 2: T+3=W,O+1=P,P+3=S. Step 3: WPS correct.
Step 1: All +1. Step 2: F+1=G,I+1=J,R+1=S,E+1=F. Step 3: GJSF correct.
Step 1: Mirror 27-pos. Step 2: E(5)->22=V,F(6)->21=U,G(7)->20=T,H(8)->19=S. Step 3: VUTS correct.
Step 1: Mirror 27-pos. Step 2: L(12)->15=O,I(9)->18=R,L(12)->15=O,Y(25)->2=B. Step 3: OROB correct.

mcqs.q: If PINK=QJOL and BALL=CBMM, then RING=?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: All +1. Step 2: R+1=S,I+1=J,N+1=O,G+1=H. Step 3: SJOH correct.
Step 1: All +1. Step 2: E+1=F,R+1=S,A+1=B,S+1=T,E+1=F,R+1=S. Step 3: FSBTFS correct.
Step 1: All +1. Step 2: B->C,E->F,A->B,U->V,T->U,I->J,F->G,U->V,L->M. Step 3: CFBUVJGVM correct.
Step 1: K->N(+3),I->G(-2),T->W(+3),E->H(+3). Pattern +3,-2,+3,+3. Step 2: B+3=E,I-2=G,R+3=U,D+3=G. Step 3: EGUG correct.
Step 1: All +1: L->M,A->B,P->Q,T->U,O->P,P->Q. Step 2: M->N,O->P,B->C,I->J,L->M,E->F. Step 3: NPCJMF correct.

mcqs.q: If star=1, circle=2, triangle=3 then star+circle=?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: star=1, circle=2. Step 2: star+circle = 1+2 = 3. Step 3: 3 correct.

mcqs.q: If MOON=NPPO, what is the code for MOON? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: M+1=N, O+1=P, O+1=P, N+1=O. Step 2: MOON becomes NPPO. Step 3: NPPO correct.

mcqs.q: If TOP=UPQ, what is the code for TOP? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: T+1=U, O+1=P, P+1=Q. Step 2: TOP becomes UPQ. Step 3: UPQ correct.

mcqs.q: If DEER=EFFS, what is the code for DEER? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: D+1=E, E+1=F, E+1=F, R+1=S. Step 2: DEER becomes EFFS. Step 3: EFFS correct.

mcqs.q: If ERASER=FSBTFS, what is the code for ERASER? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: E+1=F, R+1=S, A+1=B, S+1=T, E+1=F, R+1=S. Step 2: ERASER becomes FSBTFS. Step 3: FSBTFS correct.

mcqs.q: If FISH=GJTI, what is the code for FISH? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: F+1=G, I+1=J, S+1=T, H+1=I. Step 2: FISH becomes GJTI. Step 3: GJTI correct.

mcqs.q: If GOAT=HPBU, what is the code for GOAT? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: G+1=H, O+1=P, A+1=B, T+1=U. Step 2: GOAT becomes HPBU. Step 3: HPBU correct.

mcqs.q: If WATER=XBUFS, what is the code for WATER? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: W+1=X, A+1=B, T+1=U, E+1=F, R+1=S. Step 2: WATER becomes XBUFS. Step 3: XBUFS correct.

mcqs.q: If SUN=TVO, what is the code for SUN? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: S+1=T, U+1=V, N+1=O. Step 2: SUN becomes TVO. Step 3: TVO correct.

mcqs.q: If STAR=TUBS, what is the code for STAR? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: S+1=T, T+1=U, A+1=B, R+1=S. Step 2: STAR becomes TUBS. Step 3: TUBS correct.

mcqs.q: If PENCIL=QFODJM, what is the code for PENCIL? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: P+1=Q, E+1=F, N+1=O, C+1=D, I+1=J, L+1=M. Step 2: PENCIL becomes QFODJM. Step 3: QFODJM correct.

mcqs.q: If RING=SJOH, what is the code for RING? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: R+1=S, I+1=J, N+1=O, G+1=H. Step 2: RING becomes SJOH. Step 3: SJOH correct.

mcqs.q: If PINK=QJOL, what is the code for PINK? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: P+1=Q, I+1=J, N+1=O, K+1=L. Step 2: PINK becomes QJOL. Step 3: QJOL correct.

mcqs.q: If MOBILE=NPCJMF, what is the code for MOBILE? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: M+1=N, O+1=P, B+1=C, I+1=J, L+1=M, E+1=F. Step 2: MOBILE becomes NPCJMF. Step 3: NPCJMF correct.

mcqs.q: If PEN=QFO, what is the code for PEN? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: P+1=Q, E+1=F, N+1=O. Step 2: PEN becomes QFO. Step 3: QFO correct.

mcqs.q: If BIRD=CJSE, what is the code for BIRD? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: B+1=C, I+1=J, R+1=S, D+1=E. Step 2: BIRD becomes CJSE. Step 3: CJSE correct.

mcqs.q: If SNAKE=TOBLF, what is the code for SNAKE? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: S+1=T, N+1=O, A+1=B, K+1=L, E+1=F. Step 2: SNAKE becomes TOBLF. Step 3: TOBLF correct.

mcqs.q: If CAT=DBU, what is the code for CAT? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: C+1=D, A+1=B, T+1=U. Step 2: CAT becomes DBU. Step 3: DBU correct.

mcqs.q: If TIGER=UJHFS, what is the code for TIGER? (forward +1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter +1: T+1=U, I+1=J, G+1=H, E+1=F, R+1=S. Step 2: TIGER becomes UJHFS. Step 3: UJHFS correct.

mcqs.q: If TREE=SQDD, what is the code for TREE? (backward -1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter -1: T-1=S, R-1=Q, E-1=D, E-1=D. Step 2: TREE becomes SQDD. Step 3: SQDD correct.

mcqs.q: If TOP=SNO, what is the code for TOP? (backward -1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter -1: T-1=S, O-1=N, P-1=O. Step 2: TOP becomes SNO. Step 3: SNO correct.

mcqs.q: If SUN=RTM, what is the code for SUN? (backward -1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter -1: S-1=R, U-1=T, N-1=M. Step 2: SUN becomes RTM. Step 3: RTM correct.

mcqs.q: If CODE=BNCD, what is the code for CODE? (backward -1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter -1: C-1=B, O-1=N, D-1=C, E-1=D. Step 2: CODE becomes BNCD. Step 3: BNCD correct.

mcqs.q: If FROG=EQNF, what is the code for FROG? (backward -1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter -1: F-1=E, R-1=Q, O-1=N, G-1=F. Step 2: FROG becomes EQNF. Step 3: EQNF correct.

mcqs.q: If WOLF=VNKE, what is the code for WOLF? (backward -1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter -1: W-1=V, O-1=N, L-1=K, F-1=E. Step 2: WOLF becomes VNKE. Step 3: VNKE correct.

mcqs.q: If WATER=VZSDQ, what is the code for WATER? (backward -1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter -1: W-1=V, A-1=Z, T-1=S, E-1=D, R-1=Q. Step 2: WATER becomes VZSDQ. Step 3: VZSDQ correct.

mcqs.q: If DUCK=CTBJ, what is the code for DUCK? (backward -1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter -1: D-1=C, U-1=T, C-1=B, K-1=J. Step 2: DUCK becomes CTBJ. Step 3: CTBJ correct.

mcqs.q: If STAR=RSZQ, what is the code for STAR? (backward -1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter -1: S-1=R, T-1=S, A-1=Z, R-1=Q. Step 2: STAR becomes RSZQ. Step 3: RSZQ correct.

mcqs.q: If LEAF=KDZE, what is the code for LEAF? (backward -1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter -1: L-1=K, E-1=D, A-1=Z, F-1=E. Step 2: LEAF becomes KDZE. Step 3: KDZE correct.

mcqs.q: If CALL=BZKK, what is the code for CALL? (backward -1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter -1: C-1=B, A-1=Z, L-1=K, L-1=K. Step 2: CALL becomes BZKK. Step 3: BZKK correct.

mcqs.q: If LAMB=KZLA, what is the code for LAMB? (backward -1)
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Each letter -1: L-1=K, A-1=Z, M-1=L, B-1=A. Step 2: LAMB becomes KZLA. Step 3: KZLA correct.

Step 1: Pattern [+3,+1,+3,+3] from LAP: L->O, A->B, P->S. Step 2: Apply [+3,+1,+3,+3] to DUCK: D+3=G, U+1=V, C+3=F, K+3=N. Step 3: GVFN correct.
Step 1: Pattern [+3,+1,+3,+3] from LAP: L->O, A->B, P->S. Step 2: Apply [+3,+1,+3,+3] to LION: L+3=O, I+1=J, O+3=R, N+3=Q. Step 3: OJRQ correct.
Step 1: Pattern [+3,+1,+3,+3] from LAP: L->O, A->B, P->S. Step 2: Apply [+3,+1,+3,+3] to WOLF: W+3=Z, O+1=P, L+3=O, F+3=I. Step 3: ZPOI correct.
Step 1: Pattern [+3,+1,+3,+3] from LAP: L->O, A->B, P->S. Step 2: Apply [+3,+1,+3,+3] to RING: R+3=U, I+1=J, N+3=Q, G+3=J. Step 3: UJQJ correct.
Step 1: Pattern [+3,+1,+3,+3] from LAP: L->O, A->B, P->S. Step 2: Apply [+3,+1,+3,+3] to FROG: F+3=I, R+1=S, O+3=R, G+3=J. Step 3: ISRJ correct.
Step 1: Pattern [+3,+1,+3,+3] from LAP: L->O, A->B, P->S. Step 2: Apply [+3,+1,+3,+3] to DEER: D+3=G, E+1=F, E+3=H, R+3=U. Step 3: GFHU correct.
Step 1: Pattern [+3,+1,+3,+3] from LAP: L->O, A->B, P->S. Step 2: Apply [+3,+1,+3,+3] to STAR: S+3=V, T+1=U, A+3=D, R+3=U. Step 3: VUDU correct.
Step 1: Pattern [+3,+1,+3,+3] from LAP: L->O, A->B, P->S. Step 2: Apply [+3,+1,+3,+3] to FISH: F+3=I, I+1=J, S+3=V, H+3=K. Step 3: IJVK correct.
Step 1: Pattern [+3,+1,+3,+3] from LAP: L->O, A->B, P->S. Step 2: Apply [+3,+1,+3,+3] to BOOK: B+3=E, O+1=P, O+3=R, K+3=N. Step 3: EPRN correct.

mcqs.q: If WOLF=DLOU, what is the mirror code of WOLF?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Mirror each letter (27-pos): W(23)->D(4), O(15)->L(12), L(12)->O(15), F(6)->U(21). Step 2: WOLF -> DLOU. Step 3: DLOU correct.

mcqs.q: If LILY=OROB, what is the mirror code of LILY?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Mirror each letter (27-pos): L(12)->O(15), I(9)->R(18), L(12)->O(15), Y(25)->B(2). Step 2: LILY -> OROB. Step 3: OROB correct.

mcqs.q: If HORSE=SLIHV, what is the mirror code of HORSE?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Mirror each letter (27-pos): H(8)->S(19), O(15)->L(12), R(18)->I(9), S(19)->H(8), E(5)->V(22). Step 2: HORSE -> SLIHV. Step 3: SLIHV correct.

mcqs.q: If BALL=YZOO, what is the mirror code of BALL?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Mirror each letter (27-pos): B(2)->Y(25), A(1)->Z(26), L(12)->O(15), L(12)->O(15). Step 2: BALL -> YZOO. Step 3: YZOO correct.

mcqs.q: If TIGER=GRTVI, what is the mirror code of TIGER?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Mirror each letter (27-pos): T(20)->G(7), I(9)->R(18), G(7)->T(20), E(5)->V(22), R(18)->I(9). Step 2: TIGER -> GRTVI. Step 3: GRTVI correct.

mcqs.q: If SNAKE=HMZPV, what is the mirror code of SNAKE?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Mirror each letter (27-pos): S(19)->H(8), N(14)->M(13), A(1)->Z(26), K(11)->P(16), E(5)->V(22). Step 2: SNAKE -> HMZPV. Step 3: HMZPV correct.

mcqs.q: If CAN=XZM, what is the mirror code of CAN?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Mirror each letter (27-pos): C(3)->X(24), A(1)->Z(26), N(14)->M(13). Step 2: CAN -> XZM. Step 3: XZM correct.

mcqs.q: If LAMB=OZNY, what is the mirror code of LAMB?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Mirror each letter (27-pos): L(12)->O(15), A(1)->Z(26), M(13)->N(14), B(2)->Y(25). Step 2: LAMB -> OZNY. Step 3: OZNY correct.

mcqs.q: If PENCIL=KVMXRO, what is the mirror code of PENCIL?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Mirror each letter (27-pos): P(16)->K(11), E(5)->V(22), N(14)->M(13), C(3)->X(24), I(9)->R(18), L(12)->O(15). Step 2: PENCIL -> KVMXRO. Step 3: KVMXRO correct.

mcqs.q: If DEER=WVVI, what is the mirror code of DEER?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Mirror each letter (27-pos): D(4)->W(23), E(5)->V(22), E(5)->V(22), R(18)->I(9). Step 2: DEER -> WVVI. Step 3: WVVI correct.

mcqs.q: If PEN=KVM, what is the mirror code of PEN?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Mirror each letter (27-pos): P(16)->K(11), E(5)->V(22), N(14)->M(13). Step 2: PEN -> KVM. Step 3: KVM correct.

mcqs.q: If LION=ORLM, what is the mirror code of LION?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Mirror each letter (27-pos): L(12)->O(15), I(9)->R(18), O(15)->L(12), N(14)->M(13). Step 2: LION -> ORLM. Step 3: ORLM correct.
